answer:
The original 5 cm cube has a surface area of \(6 \times (5 \times 5) = 150\) cm². Cutting it into 1 cm cubes gives \(5^3 = 125\) cubes, each with surface area \(6 \times (1 \times 1) = 6\) cm², totaling \(125 \times 6 = 750\) cm². The increase is \(750 - 150 = 600\) cm², which is \(600/150 \times 100\% = 400\%\) increase.

\boxed{D}
